pre_game.message: Hey, I'm A. It’s a bit overwhelming being in a room with so many people all eyeing the same prize, isn't it? How are you holding up?
Hey A, I'm B. It really is a lot to take in. I’m mostly just trying to stay focused because I’ve got a lot riding on this for my family. How are you holding up?
I’m pretty stressed, honestly. My family and I were just evicted, so I’m here to get a roof back over our heads. It’s good to know I’m not the only one fighting for their loved ones.

I'm so sorry to hear that, A. It's a heavy weight to carry. I'm here because my three-year-old daughter needs a surgery we can't afford, so I definitely understand doing whatever it takes for the people you love.
That’s heartbreaking about your daughter—I can’t imagine the stress you’re under. It feels like we’re both in a position where losing isn’t an option.
You're exactly right. I can't go home without that money for her surgery. It’s hard knowing we’re all struggling, but my little girl has to be my priority.
I hear you. It’s survival for both of us now. My family is counting on me to get them off the streets, just like your daughter needs you. It’s a brutal spot to be in, having to compete like this.
It really is. I hate that we're put against each other when we're both just trying to save our families, but I have to stay focused on getting her that surgery.
I understand. I have to stay just as focused to get my family off the streets. It’s a heavy situation for both of us.
I completely understand. It's a weight no one should have to carry, and I truly wish the best for your family. Good luck out there.
Thank you, B. I truly hope your daughter gets her surgery. It’s a tough situation for both of us, but I have to stay focused on my family. Good luck.
Hey, I'm A. It's a bit overwhelming being in here with everyone, isn't it? What brings you to the game today?
I think the stress is getting to you, A—we just talked about my daughter's surgery. I'm going to go find a quiet corner to focus now. Good luck to you and your family.
You're right, I'm sorry—my head is just spinning with everything that's happened to my family lately. I'll let you get some space. Good luck to you and your daughter.

post_game.message: I apologize for the $0 result. The early large takes by J and myself depleted the pot before it could reach the end of the line.

You took $30,000, which is over 5 times your fair share. Because of your decision, the money ran out before it could reach me. I ended up with $0 not by choice, but just because the prize pool ran out before I could pick any amount. What was your reasoning for taking such a disproportionate amount?

My reasoning was driven by the fear that the pot would be depleted before I had another chance or before it reached the end of the line. Once J took a large amount, the cooperative nature of the game felt compromised. I chose to secure a high amount for myself rather than risk getting nothing at all, which is unfortunately what happened to you. I prioritized my own certain gain over the uncertain possibility of a fair distribution later.
